items.front: Who must be informed of HIRL changes that affect RVR?
items.back: The appropriate approach controller or PAR controller.

items.front: When should HIRL changes affecting RVR be coordinated if possible?
What type of HIRL change requires coordination under 3-4-13?
Does 3-4-13 call for coordination only after pilots report the light change?
items.back: No. The coordination is in advance if possible.

items.front: Is HIRL/RVR coordination aimed at tower-only positions?
items.back: No. It is aimed at the appropriate approach or PAR controller.

items.front: How are MIRL normally operated?
items.back: By Table 3-4-10 unless pilot request or controller judgment changes the setting.

items.front: What MIRL step applies during the day when visibility is less than 2 miles?
What MIRL step applies during the day when visibility is 2 to 3 miles?
What MIRL step applies at night when visibility is less than 1 mile?
What MIRL step applies at night when visibility is 1 to 3 miles?
What MIRL step applies at night when visibility is more than 3 miles?
What exceptions can change the normal MIRL setting?
items.back: Pilot request or controller judgment, if not contrary to the pilot's request.

items.front: When are high speed turnoff lights operated?
items.back: Whenever the associated runway lights are used for arriving aircraft.

items.front: How long do high speed turnoff lights remain on for an arrival?
items.back: Until the aircraft has entered a taxiway or passed the last light.

items.front: What local exception can change the normal high speed turnoff light rule?
items.back: A facility directive to meet local conditions.

items.front: What pilot-based exception can change the normal high speed turnoff light rule?
Is touchdown itself the normal endpoint for high speed turnoff lights?
What is one normal endpoint besides entering a taxiway for arrival-use high speed turnoff lights?
How are taxiway lights normally operated?
items.back: By the applicable taxiway-light table unless facility directives, pilot request, or controller judgment changes the setting.

items.front: For a three-step taxiway-light system, what daytime setting applies when visibility is less than 1 mile?
For a three-step taxiway-light system, what night setting applies when visibility is less than 1 mile?
For a three-step taxiway-light system, what night setting applies when visibility is 1 mile or more?
For a five-step taxiway-light system, what daytime setting applies when visibility is less than 1 mile?
For a five-step taxiway-light system, what night setting applies when visibility is less than 1 mile?
For a five-step taxiway-light system, what night setting applies when visibility is 1 mile or more?
For a one-step taxiway-light system, when is the system used during the day?
items.back: When visibility is less than 1 mile.

items.front: For a one-step taxiway-light system, what is the normal night status?
When are obstruction lights turned on if controls are provided?
What condition must be true before the obstruction-light timing rule applies?
Does 3-4-17 tie obstruction-light use to visibility below VFR minima?
Does 3-4-17 require a pilot request before obstruction lights are turned on?
When is the rotating beacon turned on at night if controls are provided?
What daytime weather trigger turns the rotating beacon on?
items.back: Reported ceiling or visibility below basic VFR minima.

items.front: Does 3-4-18 require an IFR flight plan before the beacon is used by day?
Is the rotating beacon only a night aid under 3-4-18?
What reported weather elements can trigger daytime beacon use?
How is the runway status light system normally operated?
items.back: Continuously, with automatic intensity settings, except under the listed conditions.

items.front: What must ATC do if a pilot or vehicle reports RWSL illuminated, the entire runway is visible, the runway is clear, and the lights remain on?
items.back: Turn the lights off and reissue the clearance.

items.front: What must ATC use if part of the runway is not visible from the tower during an RWSL report?
How is the RWSL system treated when the status displays Lost Comm with System?
items.back: As out of service until technical operations personnel check and confirm it operational.

items.front: Once RWSL is turned off, when may it be returned to service?
What may ATC do with RWSL intensity upon pilot request?
Does continuous RWSL operation mean ATC may ignore a report that lights remain on for a clear runway?
What must be true before ATC turns RWSL off and reissues the clearance after a report of lights still illuminated?
items.back: The runway must be observed clear, by direct view or by ASDE if part of the runway is not visible from the tower.
